doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_buildopt_float16_p: build-time predicate reporting whether libmpfr was compiled with _Float16 / IEEE 754 binary16 support (MPFR_WANT_FLOAT16). C returns int (0 or 1) per a preprocessor branch; TS returns boolean. The TS port has no relied-upon native binary16 type (Float16Array is a stage-3 TC39 proposal not universally available across Bun and Node engine versions we target), so the value is the compile-time constant `false`. The libmpfr-side observable depends on how the system libmpfr was built; the golden_driver emits the TS-expected `false` unconditionally because the TS port does not (and cannot) advertise binary16 support. Class 'misc' (1000ms per-case budget). Tag minimums (happy>=20, edge>=30, adversarial>=10, fuzz>=50, mined>=5) are inapplicable to no-arg accessor functions whose input domain is empty -- a single happy case is the entire test surface.
divergence_from_c: C returns int (0 or 1) from a preprocessor branch on MPFR_WANT_FLOAT16; TS returns boolean and is unconditionally `false`. Pure-TS has no relied-upon binary16 type -- the typed-array surface we depend on is f32/f64, and Float16Array remains a stage-3 proposal whose presence is not guaranteed on either Bun >= 1.3 or Node >= 22 -- so advertising binary16 support would be a lie. If/when Float16Array is universally adopted and we wire it in, this flips to `true` and the golden is regenerated. The golden_driver does NOT call libmpfr's mpfr_buildopt_float16_p() for the expected output (its return depends on the system libmpfr build flags) -- it hard-codes `false` to match the TS-port contract.
refs: mpfr/src/buildopt.c L35-L43 -- the C reference (mpfr_buildopt_float16_p preprocessor branch).
CLAUDE.md Rule 7 -- tag minimums (carved out here per the spec.doc note above).
CLAUDE.md Rule 12 -- pure-TS port runs on Bun OR Node; no relied-upon binary16 in either.
